questions.question: Which programming language is known as the language of the web?
What does HTML stand for?
Which of the following is NOT a feature introduced in ECMAScript 6 (ES6)?
In JavaScript, what is the purpose of the 'Array.prototype.map()' method?
options: To filter out specific elements from an array
To create a new array with the results of calling a function on every element
To sort the elements of an array in place
To check if at least one element passes a test
questions.correctAnswer: To create a new array with the results of calling a function on every element

questions.question: What is the time complexity of searching in a perfectly balanced Binary Search Tree (BST)?
Which of the JavaScript statements creates a deep copy of a nested object?
Which CSS property is used to change the background color of an element?
What is the correct way to write a JavaScript array?
Which keyword is used to define a constant variable in ES6?
What is the default value of the 'position' property in CSS?
Which operator is used to compare both value and type in JavaScript?
What does 'Hoisting' refer to in JavaScript?
options: Moving variables to the bottom of the script
Variables and function declarations are moved to the top of their scope
Executing the script line by line
questions.correctAnswer: Variables and function declarations are moved to the top of their scope

questions.question: What is a 'closure' in JavaScript?
options: A way to close a browser window
A function that has access to its outer function scope even after the outer function has returned
questions.correctAnswer: A function that has access to its outer function scope even after the outer function has returned

questions.question: What is the purpose of the alt attribute for images in HTML?
options: To make the image look better
To provide alternative text if the image cannot be displayed
To create a link for the image
questions.correctAnswer: To provide alternative text if the image cannot be displayed

Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In First Out)?
What is the main advantage of using 'async/await' over Promises in JavaScript?
options: It makes asynchronous code look and behave more like synchronous code
It is only available in modern browsers
questions.correctAnswer: It makes asynchronous code look and behave more like synchronous code

questions.question: Which HTML element is used for the largest heading?
What is the purpose of 'use strict' in JavaScript?
options: To enforce stricter parsing and error handling
questions.correctAnswer: To enforce stricter parsing and error handling

questions.question: What is the primary function of the 'finally' block in a try-catch statement?
options: To provide an alternate execution path
To execute code regardless of exceptions
questions.correctAnswer: To execute code regardless of exceptions

questions.question: Which data structure follows the LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) principle?
What is the purpose of the 'git stash' command in Git version control?
options: To push changes to a remote repository
